questions.text: What does 'Al-Inshiqaq' mean?
questions.answer: The Splitting Open (of the sky).

questions.text: What is the sky's response to its Lord's command?
questions.answer: It listens/obeys and is obligated to do so (Adhinat).

questions.text: What will happen to the earth in Verse 3?
questions.answer: It will be stretched out (Muddat).

questions.text: What will the earth do with what is inside it?
questions.answer: It will throw out what is in it and become empty.

questions.text: How is the human's life journey described in Verse 6?
questions.answer: As a 'laboring toward your Lord with [great] exertion' until you meet Him.

questions.text: What is the sign of a 'light' reckoning for a person?
questions.answer: He will be given his record in his right hand.

questions.text: Where will the one given his book in the right hand return?
questions.answer: To his people (in Paradise) rejoicing.

questions.text: How will the 'unsuccessful' person receive his record?
questions.answer: Behind his back.

questions.text: What will the person who receives his book behind his back cry out for?
questions.answer: Destruction (Thubura).

questions.text: Why did the loser think he would never return to Allah?
questions.answer: Because he was rejoicing among his people in the world and thought he would not be held accountable.

questions.text: Allah swears by the 'twilight'. What is the Arabic term used?
questions.answer: Al-Shafaq.

questions.text: What does 'the moon when it becomes full' symbolize in Verse 18?
questions.answer: The completion of stages or the transition of states.

questions.text: What is meant by 'you will embark upon stage after stage' (Tabaqan 'an Tabaq)?
questions.answer: Mankind will pass through various states: from life to death, to the grave, to resurrection.

questions.text: Why does Allah criticize the disbelievers regarding the Quran?
questions.answer: Because they do not prostrate when the Quran is recited to them.

questions.text: Does Allah know what the disbelievers keep within themselves?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Allah is most knowing of what they keep within themselves' (Verse 23).

questions.text: What 'tidings' are given to the disbelievers?
questions.answer: Tidings of a painful punishment.

questions.text: Who is exempted from the painful punishment?
questions.answer: Those who believe and do righteous deeds.

questions.text: What is the reward for the believers mentioned at the end?
questions.answer: A reward uninterrupted (Ghayru Mamnun).

questions.text: What is the spiritual significance of the sky 'listening' to Allah?
questions.answer: It shows that inanimate creation is perfectly submissive to Allah, while humans have been given a choice.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the meeting with Allah?
questions.answer: As an inevitable conclusion to the human's hard work and effort in this life.
